page: Microsoft Azure F80als_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Falsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 80 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD Zen 9V45 processor with an x86_64 architecture operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The system is configured with 160.0 GB of system memory, resulting in a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is capable of utilizing Premium Storage.

In benchmark evaluations, the F80als_v7 demonstrates top-tier performance across multiple compute and memory metrics. The 80-core AMD Zen processor achieves a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 290,605.12 and a single-core score of 3,780.35, both placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Memory bandwidth is also highly rated, with cached small-block read speeds reaching 14,244.96 GB/sec and uncached large-block write speeds at 213.01 GB/sec. Large-scale data operations are supported by a PassMark database score of 41,056.88 and Redis SET throughput of 199.48 million operations per second.

The primary tradeoff of the F80als_v7 is its resource allocation profile. While it delivers high compute density and high clock speeds, the 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU limits its suitability for memory-intensive applications. The lack of local storage and GPU accelerators further narrows its focus to pure CPU-bound tasks. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the instance maximizes compute power per resource unit for workloads that do not require massive memory footprints or local disk space. It is suited for compute-heavy workloads such as batch processing, web serving, data decompression, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure F80als_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the Falsv7 family, featuring 80 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD Zen 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 160.0 GB of RAM, providing a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators but supports Premium Storage. Benchmark results place the F80als_v7 in the top tier for both single-core and multi-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 290,605.12 and a PassMark CPU score of 128,254.89. It also demonstrates high throughput in data decompression and Redis operations. This configuration is designed for CPU-bound workloads, including web serving, batch processing, and CPU-based machine learning inference, where high compute density is required without a large memory footprint.

tagline: An 80-vCPU compute-optimized virtual machine featuring high-frequency AMD Zen processors and a low memory-to-core ratio for CPU-intensive workloads.

160.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core.
